concepts.name: Doubly Linked List
concepts.short_definition: A linear data structure where each node points to both the next and previous nodes.

concepts.name: Node Structure
concepts.short_definition: The fundamental building block of a doubly linked list, containing data and pointers to adjacent nodes.

concepts.name: Head Pointer
concepts.short_definition: A reference to the first node in the doubly linked list.

concepts.name: Tail Pointer
concepts.short_definition: A reference to the last node in the doubly linked list.

concepts.name: Insertion
concepts.short_definition: The process of adding a new node into the doubly linked list at various positions.

concepts.name: Deletion
concepts.short_definition: The process of removing an existing node from the doubly linked list.

concepts.name: Traversal
concepts.short_definition: Visiting each node in the doubly linked list, typically from head to tail or tail to head.

concepts.name: Forward Traversal
concepts.short_definition: Traversing the list by following the 'next' pointers from the head.

concepts.name: Backward Traversal
concepts.short_definition: Traversing the list by following the 'previous' pointers from the tail.

concepts.name: Memory Management
concepts.short_definition: Handling the creation and deallocation of nodes in the doubly linked list.
